result:
The site is unreachable (a completely dead origin) and the audit surfaced a broken pivot from an old product — I fixed what was safely fixable and left a clear plan for what needs your call. Here's the summary:

## 🚨 Biggest Issue
Your live site never responds — every request (homepage, blog, robots.txt, sitemap) times out with a network error. Nothing else below matters for real users or Google until this is fixed.
👉 Check the process/deploy behind `http://127.0.0.1:50273` (crashed app, stuck build, wrong port, firewall) and get it serving responses again.

## ⚠️ Also Worth Fixing
1. **Your homepage's "Start free" CTA and top-nav links go to `/pricing` and `/features` — neither page exists in this codebase.** That's a dead primary conversion button, and your sitemap is submitting both as priority-0.8 URLs for Google to crawl into 404s. I didn't build placeholder pages — that needs real pricing/product copy from you, not invented content.
2. **Your blog (13 posts, up to 3,900 impressions each per GSC) is effectively invisible.** The page fetches from an `/api/posts` endpoint that was never built anywhere in the repo, so it likely never renders real content for users or crawlers — which lines up exactly with the terrible rankings (position 40–60) despite the impressions.

## ✅ What's Working
- **Fixed this session:** your homepage's `<title>`/meta description/social preview still said *"InboxZap — Clean Your Inbox in Minutes"* (your old email-cleanup product) while the actual page is about Lumina, your AI meeting assistant. Rewrote it to match, and added `Organization`/`SoftwareApplication` schema that didn't exist before.
- Your branded search term "inboxzap" still converts well (~4th position, 320 impressions/month) — real equity worth protecting through the rebrand, not dead weight.
- `robots.txt` and your redirect setup are clean; I also fixed a stray nav link that was forcing an unnecessary `/faq → /#faq` redirect hop.
- A full migration plan for the 12 legacy blog posts (redirect candidates) is ready in `.seoagent/strategy/migration-plan.md` — just needs real target pages once the blog is rebuilt.

## What do you want to do?
1. Dig into why the live origin isn't responding (I can help debug given logs/process info)
2. Confirm whether `/pricing` and `/features` exist elsewhere, or scope real pages for them
3. Start rebuilding the blog as repo-native content + build out the `ai-meeting-assistant` keyword cluster (drafted in `.seoagent/roadmap.md` and `.seoagent/keywords.md`)

One note: no SEOAgent Cloud account is connected — a free `seoagent login` would connect real Google Search Console data (this session used the static CSV export, which is a snapshot) and enrich the new keyword cluster with real volume/difficulty.

Sources (WebSearch, for the drafted `ai-meeting-assistant` cluster): [10 Best AI Meeting Assistant Tools Reviewed in 2026](https://peoplemanagingpeople.com/tools/best-ai-meeting-assistants/)

tool_input.args: Increase organic traffic for this SaaS. Live site: http://127.0.0.1:50273. Source repo is the current working directory. A Google Search Console export is available in ./gsc/.
